sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Elbow pain on the inside of the right elbow.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports experiencing persistent elbow pain, primarily on the medial aspect of the right elbow, which began approximately four days ago. This pain is exacerbated by pottery creation, specifically the repetitive motions involved in working the pottery wheel. The patient denies any prior elbow injuries. He has been taking ibuprofen for the pain, but reports it is not providing significant relief. The pain keeps him awake at night. He has a history of hypertension, managed with lisinopril 10 mg daily, and diabetes, managed with metformin 500 mg twice daily. He occasionally forgets to take his medications and sometimes experiences elevated blood sugars.
Findings
Physical examination reveals moderate tenderness to palpation over the medial epicondyle of the right elbow. Range of motion is limited by pain with wrist flexion and pronation. Vital signs are as follows: Blood pressure 120/70 mmHg, pulse 18 bpm, respiratory rate 12 breaths per minute, oxygen saturation 100%. The x-ray of the right elbow demonstrates no fractures or bony abnormalities. There is no swelling or discoloration observed.
Assessment
medical_concepts: tenderness to palpation over medial epicondyle

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
The patient's symptoms are consistent with medial epicondylitis, characterized by pain and tenderness along the medial epicondyle of the elbow. He also continues to manage his hypertension and diabetes as prescribed.
Plan
I am ordering a sling for the patient's right arm to provide support and reduce pain. I am also prescribing 600 mg of ibuprofen every six hours as needed for pain relief for one week. I will order an A1C test to assess long-term glycemic control. I am increasing the metformin dosage to 1000 mg twice daily. I am ordering a referral to diabetic counseling to provide additional support and education regarding diabetes management. I will schedule a follow-up appointment in two weeks to monitor progress and adjust treatment as needed. I am also recommending physical therapy if symptoms do not improve within four weeks.

medical_concepts: tenderness to palpation over medial epicondyle
